name: execute_code
description:
Write and execute TypeScript code in a sandboxed Bun subprocess. Use for data processing, file analysis, bulk transformations, and any task requiring programmatic logic beyond shell one-liners.

File access is restricted to SESSION_DIR via the injected `tools` object:
- tools.readFile(path) → string
- tools.readJson(path) → parsed JSON
- tools.writeFile(path, content) → void
- tools.listDir(path) → string[]
- tools.exists(path) → boolean
- tools.stat(path) → { isFile, isDirectory, size }
- tools.mkdir(path) → void

All paths must be within SESSION_DIR. Use console.log() for output — it becomes the tool result. For large results, write to a file and log the path.

Before executing code that processes external data, first use other tools (web, bash) to download/prepare data into the session directory.

Output is capped at 20KB. Execution timeout is configurable (default 30s, max 120s).

code.description: TypeScript code to execute. Use the injected `tools` object for file I/O (tools.readFile, tools.writeFile, etc.). All file paths must be relative to or within SESSION_DIR. Use console.log() for output.

description.description: Brief human-readable description of what this code does. Logged for audit.

timeout.description: Execution timeout in milliseconds. Clamped to [1000, 120000]. Defaults to 30000 (30s).
